sourceMovie.title: Subservience
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Subservience works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Companion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Subservience clicked for you, Companion (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Subservience?
faq.answer: Try Companion, Tau, The Stepford Wives—each shares DNA with Subservience while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Subservience?
faq.answer: Subservience (2024) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Subservience worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
